128	
129	@dataclass(frozen=True)
130	class Event:
131	    """An OBSERVATION. Immutable, ordered, and never a conclusion."""
132	    seq: int
133	    unit: str                    # what this is about — a DOI, a coverage cell id, a route id
134	    kind: str
135	    actor: str                   # which component observed it (for provenance, not for authority)
136	    payload: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
137	    ts: float = field(default_factory=time.time)
138	
139	    def to_json(self) -> str:
140	        return json.dumps({'seq': self.seq, 'unit': self.unit, 'kind': self.kind,
141	                           'actor': self.actor, 'payload': self.payload, 'ts': self.ts},
142	                          sort_keys=True)
143	
144	
145	def _scan_for_conclusions(kind: str, payload: dict) -> list[str]:
146	    """Return the reasons this payload is an ASSERTION rather than an OBSERVATION."""
147	    bad: list[str] = []
148	    for k, v in payload.items():
149	        kl = str(k).lower()
150	        if kl in RESERVED_KEYS:
151	            bad.append(f'key {k!r} IS a label — the reducer derives it; a component may not set it')
152	            continue
153	        if kl in VERBATIM_KEYS:
154	            continue                       # raw text from the world. It may say anything.
155	        if isinstance(v, str):
156	            vl = v.lower().strip()
157	            for r in RESERVED_VALUES:
158	                # match the whole value or a word-bounded occurrence — not a substring of a word
159	                if vl == r or re.search(rf'(?<![a-z0-9_]){re.escape(r)}(?![a-z0-9_])', vl):
160	                    bad.append(f'{k}={v!r} states the conclusion {r!r}; emit the OBSERVATION instead '
161	                               f'(word_count / http_status / byline), and let the reducer conclude')
162	                    break
163	    return bad
164	
165	
166	class Ledger:
167	    """Append-only. You may add an observation. You may not edit, delete, or conclude."""
168	
169	    def __init__(self, path: Path | None = None):
170	        self._events: list[Event] = []
171	        self._path = path
172	        self._seq = 0
173	
174	    # -- write side: exactly one method, and it is guarded -------------------------------------
175	    def emit(self, unit: str, kind: str, actor: str, **payload) -> Event:
176	        if kind not in EventKind.ALL:
177	            raise ForbiddenLabel(f'unknown event kind {kind!r}')
178	        problems = _scan_for_conclusions(kind, payload)
179	        if problems:
180	            raise ForbiddenLabel(
181	                f'{actor} tried to write a CONCLUSION into the ledger:\n    ' + '\n    '.join(problems))
182	        return self._append(unit, kind, actor, payload)
183	
184	    def _append(self, unit: str, kind: str, actor: str, payload: dict) -> Event:
185	        self._seq += 1
186	        ev = Event(seq=self._seq, unit=unit, kind=kind, actor=actor, payload=dict(payload))
187	        self._events.append(ev)
188	        if self._path:
189	            with open(self._path, 'a') as fh:
190	                fh.write(ev.to_json() + '\n')
191	        return ev
192	
193	    # -- the reducer's audit trail --------------------------------------------------------------
194	    def record_derivation(self, unit: str, kind: str, reducer: str, inputs: list[int],
195	                          **verdict) -> Event:
196	        """A reducer may append its own verdict — FOR AUDIT ONLY. Nobody else can call this.
197	
198	        The frame check below is not decoration. `emit()` is the door components use, and it refuses
199	        conclusions. This is the door the reducer uses, and it is bolted from the inside: if the
200	        calling frame is not this module, it raises. There is no third door.
201	
202	        Downstream reducers NEVER TRUST these records — they RE-DERIVE from the observations. A
203	        recorded verdict is an audit artifact, not an authority. That is the difference between a
204	        ledger and a cache, and caching the label is how we shipped "FULLTEXT" on a cookie banner.
205	        """
206	        import inspect
207	        caller = inspect.currentframe().f_back
208	        if caller.f_globals.get('__name__') != __name__:
209	            raise ForbiddenLabel(
210	                f'{caller.f_globals.get("__name__")!r} tried to record a DERIVED label directly. '
211	                f'Only a reducer inside {__name__} may. Emit OBSERVATIONS and call derive_*().')
212	        return self._append(unit, kind, reducer,
213	                            {**verdict, 'derived_by': reducer, 'from_events': list(inputs)})
214	
215	    # -- read side ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
216	    def events(self, unit: str | None = None, kind: str | None = None) -> list[Event]:
217	        return [e for e in self._events
218	                if (unit is None or e.unit == unit) and (kind is None or e.kind == kind)]
219	
220	    def units(self) -> list[str]:
221	        return list(dict.fromkeys(e.unit for e in self._events))
222	
223	    def __len__(self) -> int:
224	        return len(self._events)
225	
226	
227	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
228	# OBSERVERS — pure functions from raw content to OBSERVATIONS. They conclude nothing.
229	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
230	
231	_CID = re.compile(r'\(cid:\d+\)')
232	_CHROME = re.compile(
233	    r'website uses cookies|accept\s+(?:all\s+)?cookies|by clicking the "accept" button|'
234	    r'enable javascript|sign in to (?:your account|continue)|create an account|'
235	    r'this site requires|subscribe to (?:read|view)|purchase access|add to cart|'
236	    # ...the repository LANDING PAGES that fooled us: Oxford ORA, PubMed Central, generic portals.
237	    r'skip to main content|we welcome feedback|send message|research archive|'
238	    r'privacy policy|terms of use|all rights reserved|official websites use',
239	    re.I)
240	_PUBLISHED_STAMP = re.compile(r'published version|version of record|\(refereed\)|original citation', re.I)
241	_PREPRINT_STAMP = re.compile(
242	    r'nber working paper (?:series|no)|national bureau of economic research|'
243	    r'iza (?:dp|discussion paper)|this (?:draft|version):|preliminary(?: and incomplete)?|'
244	    r'do not (?:cite|quote) without|arxiv:\d', re.I)
245	
246	
247	def observe_text(text: str, header_chars: int = 1500) -> dict:
248	    """Everything the ledger needs to know about a blob of retrieved text — and NOT ONE CONCLUSION.
249	
250	    Note `header_chars`: a preprint stamp is only evidence of what a document IS when it appears in
251	    the document's OWN header. Every economics paper cites NBER working papers in its REFERENCES —
252	    scanning the whole text for "NBER Working Paper" identifies the bibliography, not the paper.
253	    (That naive scan called the actual Journal of Economic Perspectives article a working paper.)
254	    """
255	    words = text.split()
256	    header = text[:header_chars]
257	    cid_tokens = sum(1 for t in words if 'cid:' in t)
258	    stripped = _CID.sub('', text)
259	    real_words = len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{3,}', stripped))
260	    # How much REAL text is in the header? A title page of pure (cid:NN) glyph codes decodes to
261	    # nothing — and a header we cannot READ is a header we cannot CHECK AN IDENTITY AGAINST. It is
262	    # not a stranger's paper. It is an unreadable one, and those are different facts.
263	    header_real = len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{3,}', _CID.sub('', header)))
264	    return {
265	        'word_count': len(words),
266	        'char_count': len(text),
267	        'readable_word_count': real_words,
268	        'glyph_garbage_ratio': round(cid_tokens / max(1, len(words)), 4),
269	        'chrome_markers': len(_CHROME.findall(text)),
270	        'published_stamp_in_header': bool(_PUBLISHED_STAMP.search(header)),
271	        'preprint_stamp_in_header': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(header)),
272	        'preprint_stamp_anywhere': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(text)),
273	        'header_real_words': header_real,
274	        # the window identity is checked in. BOUNDED ON PURPOSE: searching the WHOLE document for an
275	        # author name matches the REFERENCES — every requested author is "present" somewhere in every
276	        # paper. (A mathematician named Parry is cited in the maths paper we misfiled under Parry
277	        # et al. A whole-text search "confirmed" the wrong paper.)
278	        'identity_window': header,
279	        'header_text': header[:400],
280	    }
281	
282	
283	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
284	# REDUCERS — the ONLY place a label may come into existence.
285	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
286	
287	# ---- 1. backend outcome ----------------------------------------------------------------------
288	
289	RESPONDED            = 'RESPONDED'                    # the backend answered with content
290	NOT_INDEXED          = 'NOT_INDEXED_BY_THIS_BACKEND'  # 404 — a fact about THEIR INDEX, not the world
291	BACKEND_FAILED       = 'BACKEND_FAILED'               # 429/503/timeout — a fact about OUR REQUEST
292	ACCESS_BLOCKED       = 'ACCESS_BLOCKED'               # 401/403/paywall — a fact about ENTITLEMENT
293	NO_OUTCOME           = 'NO_OUTCOME'                   # attempted, never came back. A HANG, not a gap.
294	
295	
296	def derive_backend_outcome(events: list[Event], adapter: str) -> str:
297	    """What actually happened to one adapter. `None` is not an answer — it is four different worlds."""
298	    mine = [e for e in events if e.payload.get('adapter') == adapter]
299	    if any(e.kind == EventKind.THROTTLED for e in mine):
300	        return BACKEND_FAILED
301	    if any(e.kind == EventKind.BLOCKED for e in mine):
302	        return ACCESS_BLOCKED
303	    for e in mine:
304	        if e.kind != EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED:
305	            continue
306	        code = e.payload.get('http_status')
307	        if code in (429, 503):
308	            return BACKEND_FAILED           # even if someone logged it as a "response"
309	        if code in (401, 403):
310	            return ACCESS_BLOCKED
311	        if code == 404:
312	            return NOT_INDEXED
313	        if e.payload.get('transport_error'):
314	            return BACKEND_FAILED           # timeout / DNS / reset / bad JSON
315	        return RESPONDED
316	    if any(e.kind == EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED for e in mine):
317	        return NO_OUTCOME
318	    return NO_OUTCOME
319	
320	
321	# ---- 2. route status -------------------------------------------------------------------------
322	
323	@dataclass(frozen=True)
324	class RouteStatus:
325	    state: str                     # UNROUTED | INCOMPLETE | COMPLETE_DEGRADED | COMPLETE
326	    planned: tuple[str, ...]
327	    outcomes: dict[str, str]
328	    missing: tuple[str, ...]       # planned adapters with NO terminal outcome
329	    failed: tuple[str, ...]        # adapters that were throttled/blocked/errored
330	    budget_stopped: bool
331	
332	    @property
333	    def supports_absence(self) -> bool:
334	        """THE question. Only an ADEQUATE route licenses "we looked, and it is not there."
335	
336	        Every planned adapter must have genuinely ANSWERED. One 429, one hang, or a budget stop and
337	        the honest label is SEARCH_FAILED — we do not know, and we may not say we do.
338	        """
339	        return (self.state == 'COMPLETE'
340	                and not self.missing and not self.failed and not self.budget_stopped
341	                and bool(self.planned))
342	
343	
344	def derive_route_status(events: list[Event]) -> RouteStatus:
345	    """route_complete means EVERY PLANNED ADAPTER HAS A TERMINAL OUTCOME RECORD.
346	
347	    It NEVER means "an adapter was mapped".
348	    """
349	    planned: list[str] = []
350	    for e in events:
351	        if e.kind == EventKind.ROUTE_PLANNED:
352	            planned += [a for a in e.payload.get('adapters', []) if a not in planned]
353	    if not planned:
354	        return RouteStatus('UNROUTED', (), {}, (), (), False)
355	
356	    budget = any(e.kind == EventKind.BUDGET_STOPPED for e in events)
357	    outcomes = {a: derive_backend_outcome(events, a) for a in planned}
358	    missing = tuple(a for a, o in outcomes.items() if o == NO_OUTCOME)
359	    failed = tuple(a for a, o in outcomes.items() if o in (BACKEND_FAILED, ACCESS_BLOCKED))
360	
361	    if missing:
362	        state = 'INCOMPLETE'
363	    elif failed or budget:
364	        state = 'COMPLETE_DEGRADED'     # every adapter has a record — but the search DID NOT WORK
365	    else:
366	        state = 'COMPLETE'
367	    return RouteStatus(state, tuple(planned), outcomes, missing, failed, budget)
368	
369	
370	# ---- 3. content profile ----------------------------------------------------------------------
371	
372	FULLTEXT_MIN = 2500      # a journal article is 5,000-20,000 words
373	ABSTRACT_MIN = 120
374	
375	C_FULLTEXT     = 'FULLTEXT'
376	C_ABSTRACT     = 'ABSTRACT'
377	C_CITATION     = 'CITATION_ONLY'
378	C_NOT_DOC      = 'NOT_A_DOCUMENT'        # a cookie banner, a login wall, a paywall interstitial
379	C_UNREADABLE   = 'UNREADABLE_ENCODING'   # a PDF whose glyphs never decoded
380	
381	
382	def derive_content_profile(events: list[Event]) -> tuple[str, dict]:
383	    """FULLTEXT is EARNED BY WORD COUNT AND READABILITY. It is never a thing a fetcher may declare."""
384	    prof = None
385	    for e in events:
386	        if e.kind == EventKind.CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED:
387	            prof = e.payload
388	    if prof is None:
389	        return C_CITATION, {'reason': 'no content was ever profiled'}
390	
391	    wc = prof.get('word_count', 0)
392	    readable = prof.get('readable_word_count', 0)
393	    chrome = prof.get('chrome_markers', 0)
394	
395	    # A cookie banner is not a short paper. It is NOT A PAPER.
396	    if chrome and wc < FULLTEXT_MIN:
397	        return C_NOT_DOC, {'reason': f'{chrome} site-chrome marker(s) in {wc}w — this is a web page, '
398	                                     f'not a document', **prof}
399	    if wc and prof.get('glyph_garbage_ratio', 0) > 0.10:
400	        return C_UNREADABLE, {'reason': f'{prof["glyph_garbage_ratio"]:.0%} of tokens are (cid:NN) '
401	                                        f'glyph codes — the font never decoded', **prof}
402	    if readable >= FULLTEXT_MIN:
403	        return C_FULLTEXT, {'reason': f'{readable:,} readable words', **prof}
404	    if max(wc, readable) >= ABSTRACT_MIN:
405	        return C_ABSTRACT, {'reason': f'{wc:,}w — an abstract, not a paper (floor {FULLTEXT_MIN:,})',
406	                            **prof}
407	    return C_CITATION, {'reason': f'{wc}w', **prof}
408	
409	
410	# ---- 4. semantic binding ---------------------------------------------------------------------
411	
412	SAME_WORK        = 'SAME_WORK'              # this IS the journal article
413	VERSION_PUBLISHED = 'VERSION_OF_PUBLISHED'  # a repository copy OF the published version
414	VERSION_PREPRINT = 'VERSION_OF_PREPRINT'    # a working paper. A DIFFERENT SOURCE until proven equal.
415	DIFFERENT_WORK   = 'DIFFERENT_WORK'         # we fetched somebody else's paper
416	UNRESOLVED       = 'UNRESOLVED_BINDING'
417	
418	
419	def _norm(s: str) -> set[str]:
420	    stop = {'the', 'a', 'an', 'of', 'and', 'for', 'in', 'on', 'to', 'from', 'with'}
421	    return {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]+', (s or '').lower()) if w not in stop and len(w) > 2}
422	
423	
424	def derive_semantic_binding(events: list[Event]) -> tuple[str, dict]:
425	    """Is the text we fetched the work we asked for? A fetcher MAY NOT ANSWER THIS ABOUT ITSELF.
426	
427	    Sol: "A predecessor working paper and later journal article may be closely related, but they are
428	    DIFFERENT SOURCES until version equivalence is proven."
429	    """
430	    fetched = None
431	    for e in events:
432	        if e.kind == EventKind.MANIFESTATION_FETCHED:
433	            fetched = e.payload
434	    prof = None
435	    for e in events:
436	        if e.kind == EventKind.CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED:
437	            prof = e.payload
438	    if fetched is None or prof is None:
439	        return UNRESOLVED, {'reason': 'nothing was fetched'}
440	
441	    # The identity window is BOUNDED (see observe_text): a whole-document scan finds every author in
442	    # the references and "confirms" anything you ask it to.
443	    window = fetched.get('document_title', '') or prof.get('identity_window', '') \
444	        or prof.get('header_text', '')
445	    want_t = _norm(fetched.get('requested_title', ''))
446	    got_t = _norm(window)
447	    want_a = {a.lower() for a in fetched.get('requested_authors', [])}
448	    byline = (fetched.get('byline', '') or window).lower()
449	
450	    overlap = len(want_t & got_t) / max(1, len(want_t))
451	    author_hit = any(a in byline for a in want_a if a)
452	    title_match = overlap >= 0.90
453	    # A SPECIFIC title (>=4 content words) matching in full is itself strong identity evidence.
454	    # A GENERIC one is not: {rise, machines} sits entirely inside {mathematics, rise, machines}.
455	    title_specific = len(want_t) >= 4
456	
457	    ev = {'title_overlap': round(overlap, 2), 'author_in_byline': author_hit,
458	          'title_content_words': len(want_t),
459	          'requested_title': fetched.get('requested_title', '')[:70],
460	          'header_text': (prof.get('header_text') or '')[:120]}
461	
462	    # ── AN UNREADABLE HEADER IS NOT A STRANGER'S PAPER. ───────────────────────────────────────
463	    # Autor, Levy & Murnane (2003) arrives as a PDF whose title page is pure (cid:NN) glyph codes:
464	    # ZERO readable words in the header, though the BODY has 10,902. An earlier version of this
465	    # reducer read "no title, no author" as DIFFERENT_WORK — turning a FONT-ENCODING FAILURE into a
466	    # confident claim about whose paper it is. That is this project's whole disease, committed by
467	    # the cure. We cannot read it, therefore we cannot bind it. Say exactly that.
468	    if prof.get('header_real_words', 0) < 10:
469	        return UNRESOLVED, {'reason': f'the header decoded to {prof.get("header_real_words", 0)} '
470	                                      f'readable words — we CANNOT READ it, so we cannot establish '
471	                                      f'identity. Not a stranger\'s paper: an unreadable one', **ev}
472	
473	    # ── IDENTITY IS CONFIRMED BY AN AUTHOR, OR BY A SPECIFIC TITLE IN FULL. ───────────────────
474	    confirmed = author_hit or (title_match and title_specific)
475	
476	    if not confirmed:
477	        # DIFFERENT_WORK is a STRONG CLAIM ("we are holding a stranger's paper") and it needs
478	        # POSITIVE evidence. Absence of a confirming author is NOT that evidence — it is absence of
479	        # evidence, and reading the two as the same is how "429" became "no free copy exists".
480	        if title_match and not title_specific:
481	            return DIFFERENT_WORK, {
482	                'reason': f'the requested title has only {len(want_t)} content word(s), and no '
483	                          f'requested author appears in the header. A {overlap:.0%} match on a '
484	                          f'GENERIC title is a TITLE COLLISION, not identity — the short title sits '
485	                          f'inside a stranger\'s longer one', **ev}
486	        return UNRESOLVED, {'reason': f'title overlap {overlap:.0%}, no requested author in the '
487	                                      f'header — identity is NOT established, so this text may not '
488	                                      f'be attributed to this source', **ev}
489	
490	    # ...an author alone is not identity either: prolific authors write many papers.
491	    if author_hit and not title_match and not title_specific:
492	        return DIFFERENT_WORK, {'reason': f'the author matches but the title does not ({overlap:.0%}) '
493	                                          f'— same author, DIFFERENT PAPER', **ev}
494	
495	    if prof.get('published_stamp_in_header'):
496	        return VERSION_PUBLISHED, {'reason': 'identity confirmed, and the header carries a '
497	                                             'published-version/refereed stamp', **ev}
498	    if prof.get('preprint_stamp_in_header'):
499	        return VERSION_PREPRINT, {'reason': 'identity confirmed, but the header carries a '
500	                                            'preprint/working-paper stamp — peer review CHANGES '
501	                                            'NUMBERS; this is a DIFFERENT SOURCE until version '
502	                                            'equivalence is proven', **ev}
503	    return SAME_WORK, {'reason': 'identity confirmed by '
504	                                 + ('author and title' if author_hit and title_match
505	                                    else 'author in the header' if author_hit
506	                                    else f'a specific {len(want_t)}-word title matched in full')
507	                                 + ', and no preprint stamp in the header', **ev}
508	
509	
510	# ---- 5. eligibility --------------------------------------------------------------------------
511	
512	ADMISSIBLE     = 'ADMISSIBLE'
513	DISCOVERY_LEAD = 'DISCOVERY_LEAD'     # Sol's word, exactly. Real text — but not attributable HERE.
514	INADMISSIBLE   = 'INADMISSIBLE'
515	
516	
517	def derive_eligibility(events: list[Event], journal_articles_only: bool = True) -> tuple[str, dict]:
518	    """May this text be attributed to THIS source, under THIS task's instruction?
519	
520	    Task 72 demands JOURNAL ARTICLES ONLY. So citing the working paper BREAKS THE INSTRUCTION, and
521	    citing the journal with the working paper's text IS FABRICATION. There is no version in which the
522	    working-paper span ships as journal evidence.
523	    """
524	    cls, cinfo = derive_content_profile(events)
525	    binding, binfo = derive_semantic_binding(events)
526	
527	    if cls == C_NOT_DOC:
528	        return INADMISSIBLE, {'reason': f'not a document ({cinfo["reason"]})',
529	                              'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
530	    if cls == C_UNREADABLE:
531	        return INADMISSIBLE, {'reason': f'unreadable ({cinfo["reason"]})',
532	                              'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
533	    if binding == DIFFERENT_WORK:
534	        return INADMISSIBLE, {'reason': f'wrong paper ({binfo["reason"]})',
535	                              'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
536	    if binding == VERSION_PREPRINT:
537	        return DISCOVERY_LEAD, {
538	            'reason': 'working-paper text. A DISCOVERY LEAD, not automatically journal-attributable '
539	                      'evidence — version equivalence is not proven'
540	                      + (' and the task demands journal articles only' if journal_articles_only else ''),
541	            'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
542	    if binding == UNRESOLVED:
543	        return INADMISSIBLE, {'reason': 'we cannot show this text is the work we cite',
544	                              'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
545	    if cls == C_CITATION:
546	        return INADMISSIBLE, {'reason': 'no content', 'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
547	    return ADMISSIBLE, {'reason': f'{binding.lower().replace("_", " ")}; {cinfo["reason"]}',
548	                        'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
549	
550	
551	# ---- 6. weight components — "high_quality" IS FORBIDDEN ---------------------------------------
552	
553	@dataclass(frozen=True)
554	class WeightComponents:
555	    """Sol: "high_quality" must render as its COMPONENTS, each with provenance.
556	
557	    There is deliberately NO `.label` and NO `__str__` that collapses to a scalar. A single word
558	    ("high quality") is exactly the kind of claim that outruns its evidence — one strong component
559	    carries the two weak ones, and nobody can see it happen.
560	    """
561	    components: dict[str, Any]
562	    provenance: dict[str, str]
563	
564	    def render(self) -> str:
565	        return ', '.join(f'{k}={v} [{self.provenance.get(k, "?")}]'
566	                         for k, v in self.components.items())
567	
568	
569	def derive_weight_components(events: list[Event]) -> WeightComponents:
570	    comps, prov = {}, {}
571	    for e in events:
572	        if e.kind != EventKind.WEIGHT_COMPONENTS_DERIVED:
573	            continue
574	        for k, v in e.payload.items():
575	            if k.endswith('_provenance'):
576	                continue
577	            comps[k] = v
578	            prov[k] = e.payload.get(f'{k}_provenance', f'observed by {e.actor}')
579	    return WeightComponents(comps, prov)
580	
581	
582	# ---- 7. coverage status ----------------------------------------------------------------------
583	
584	UNSEARCHED    = 'UNSEARCHED'
585	SEARCH_FAILED = 'SEARCH_FAILED'
586	UNROUTED      = 'UNROUTED'
587	SEARCHED_NONE = 'SEARCHED_NONE'
588	THIN          = 'THIN'
589	SUPPORTED     = 'SUPPORTED'
590	CONFLICTED    = 'CONFLICTED'
591	
592	THIN_MAX = 1     # 1 admissible source is THIN; 2+ agreeing is SUPPORTED
593	
594	
595	def derive_coverage_status(ledger: 'Ledger', cell: str, thin_max: int = THIN_MAX) -> tuple[str, dict]:
596	    """The seven-way distinction. Collapsing any two of these is how we lied about the world.
597	
598	    Only SEARCHED_NONE — after ADEQUATE route completion — supports a scoped absence statement.
599	    CONFLICTED and THIN directly support "the literature does not settle this", which is a CORRECT
600	    ANSWER, not a failure.
601	
602	    NOTE THE SIGNATURE. It takes the LEDGER, not a flat event list, because it must go and RE-DERIVE
603	    the eligibility of every candidate source from that source's OWN observations. It will not read
604	    an `admissible` flag — no component is allowed to write one, and a reducer that trusted a stored
605	    verdict would re-open the exact hole this module closes.
606	    """
607	    events = ledger.events(cell)
608	    route = derive_route_status(events)
609	    if route.state == 'UNROUTED':
610	        return UNROUTED, {'reason': 'no route was ever planned for this cell'}
611	    if route.missing:
612	        return UNSEARCHED, {'reason': f'planned but never answered: {", ".join(route.missing)} '
613	                                      f'— an attempt with no outcome is a HANG, not an absence'}
614	    if route.budget_stopped:
615	        return SEARCH_FAILED, {'reason': 'we stopped on BUDGET. A budget stop IS NOT AN EVIDENCE GAP '
616	                                         '— we do not know what is there'}
617	    if route.failed:
618	        detail = ', '.join(f'{a}={route.outcomes[a]}' for a in route.failed)
619	        return SEARCH_FAILED, {'reason': f'the search itself failed ({detail}) — this is a fact about '
620	                                         f'OUR REQUEST, not about the literature'}
621	
622	    # Re-derive admissibility for every candidate, from that candidate's own event stream.
623	    cards, rejected = [], []
624	    for e in events:
625	        if e.kind != EventKind.CANDIDATE_IDENTIFIED or not e.payload.get('source_unit'):
626	            continue
627	        src = e.payload['source_unit']
628	        elig, einfo = derive_eligibility(ledger.events(src))
629	        if elig == ADMISSIBLE:
630	            cards.append(e)
631	        else:
632	            rejected.append((src, elig, einfo.get('reason', '')))
633	
634	    dirs = {e.payload.get('direction') for e in cards} - {None}
635	
636	    if not cards:
637	        if not route.supports_absence:
638	            return SEARCH_FAILED, {'reason': 'route did not complete adequately; absence is not '
639	                                             'established'}
640	        n = len(route.planned)
641	        if rejected:
642	            # WE RETRIEVED THINGS AND COULD NOT ADMIT THEM. That is a statement about OUR EVIDENCE,
643	            # and it must never be read as "the literature is silent". A scoped absence sentence
644	            # written off this cell has to say so out loud.
645	            detail = '; '.join(f'{s}={e} ({r})' for s, e, r in rejected[:4])
646	            return SEARCHED_NONE, {
647	                'reason': f'all {n} adapters answered, but every one of the {len(rejected)} candidate(s) '
648	                          f'retrieved was INADMISSIBLE — we hold no usable evidence. This is a fact '
649	                          f'about OUR EVIDENCE, not about the literature: {detail}',
650	                'n_sources': 0, 'n_rejected': len(rejected), 'rejected': rejected}
651	        return SEARCHED_NONE, {'reason': f'every planned adapter genuinely answered ({n}/{n}) and '
652	                                         f'returned nothing — a SCOPED ABSENCE, and the only state '
653	                                         f'that licenses one',
654	                               'n_sources': 0, 'n_rejected': 0}
655	    if len(dirs) > 1:
656	        return CONFLICTED, {'reason': f'{len(cards)} admissible sources disagree ({", ".join(sorted(dirs))}) '
657	                                      f'— "the literature does not settle this" IS THE CORRECT ANSWER',
658	                            'n_sources': len(cards), 'n_rejected': len(rejected)}
659	    if len(cards) <= thin_max:
660	        return THIN, {'reason': f'{len(cards)} admissible source — enough to report, not enough to '
661	                                f'settle', 'n_sources': len(cards), 'n_rejected': len(rejected)}
662	    return SUPPORTED, {'reason': f'{len(cards)} admissible sources agree', 'n_sources': len(cards),
663	                       'n_rejected': len(rejected)}
664	
665	
666	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
667	# The bypass detector — which labels in a corpus have NO ledger behind them?
668	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
669	
670	def find_underived_labels(row: dict) -> list[str]:
671	    """A label with no event behind it is a component asserting its own success."""
672	    out = []
673	    if 'content_status' in row:
674	        out.append(f'content_status={row["content_status"]!r} — written directly, derived from nothing')
675	    if row.get('fulltext_source'):
676	        out.append(f'fulltext_source={row["fulltext_source"]!r} — names the SCRIPT THAT RAN, '
677	                   f'not what the document IS')
678	    fw, actual = row.get('fulltext_words'), len((row.get('fulltext') or '').split())
679	    if fw and fw != actual:
680	        out.append(f'fulltext_words={fw:,} but the row holds {actual:,} — the count describes a '
681	                   f'document we no longer have')
682	    return out
683	
684	
685	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
686	# SELF-TEST — the invariants that must never regress.
687	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
688	
689	def selftest() -> int:
690	    ok = fail = 0
691	
692	    def check(name, cond, detail=''):
693	        nonlocal ok, fail
694	        if cond:
695	            ok += 1
696	            print(f'  [PASS] {name}')
697	        else:
698	            fail += 1
699	            print(f'  [FAIL] {name}\n         {detail}')
700	
701	    print('\n=== INVARIANTS ===')
702	
703	    # 1. A component cannot write the label. At all.
704	    L = Ledger()
705	    try:
706	        L.emit('d', EventKind.CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED, 'wp_fetch', content_status='FULLTEXT')
707	        check('a component CANNOT write content_status directly', False, 'IT WAS ACCEPTED')
708	    except ForbiddenLabel:
709	        check('a component CANNOT write content_status directly', True)
710	
711	    try:
712	        L.emit('d', EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, 'deep_fetch', note='no free copy exists')
713	        check('a component CANNOT assert "no free copy exists"', False, 'IT WAS ACCEPTED')
714	    except ForbiddenLabel:
715	        check('a component CANNOT assert "no free copy exists"', True)
716	
717	    try:
718	        L.emit('d', EventKind.WEIGHT_COMPONENTS_DERIVED, 'scorer', quality='high quality')
719	        check('a component CANNOT assert "high quality"', False, 'IT WAS ACCEPTED')
720	    except ForbiddenLabel:
721	        check('a component CANNOT assert "high quality"', True)
722	
723	    # ...but the WORLD may say anything. Raw quoted text is not an assertion.
724	    try:
725	        L.emit('d', EventKind.CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED, 'observer',
726	               header_text='Full text available. This work is complete.', word_count=9000)
727	        check('raw text from the world MAY contain those words (it is quoted, not claimed)', True)
728	    except ForbiddenLabel as e:
729	        check('raw text from the world MAY contain those words', False, str(e))
730	
731	    # 1b. ...and the reducer's own door is bolted from the inside. Call it from a FOREIGN module and
732	    #     it refuses — this exec() really does run with __name__ = 'evil_component'.
733	    ns = {'__name__': 'evil_component', 'L': L, 'EventKind': EventKind}
734	    try:
735	        exec("L.record_derivation('d', EventKind.ELIGIBILITY_DECIDED, 'sneaky', [1], eligible=True)", ns)
736	        check('an outside module CANNOT call record_derivation', False, 'IT WAS ACCEPTED')
737	    except ForbiddenLabel:
738	        check('an outside module CANNOT call record_derivation (the reducer door is bolted inside)',
739	              True)
740	
741	    # 2. 429 is not absence.
742	    L = Ledger()
743	    L.emit('p1', EventKind.ROUTE_PLANNED, 'router', adapters=['s2', 'openalex'])
744	    for a in ('s2', 'openalex'):
745	        L.emit('p1', EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, 'fetch', adapter=a)
746	        L.emit('p1', EventKind.THROTTLED, 'fetch', adapter=a, http_status=429)
747	    st, info = derive_coverage_status(L, 'p1')
748	    check('HTTP 429 on every adapter -> SEARCH_FAILED (never SEARCHED_NONE)',
749	          st == SEARCH_FAILED, f'got {st}')
750	    check('a throttled route does NOT support an absence claim',
751	          not derive_route_status(L.events('p1')).supports_absence)
752	
753	    # 3. route_complete requires an OUTCOME for every planned adapter, not a mapping.
754	    L = Ledger()
755	    L.emit('p2', EventKind.ROUTE_PLANNED, 'router', adapters=['s2', 'openalex', 'arxiv'])
756	    L.emit('p2', EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, 'fetch', adapter='s2')
757	    L.emit('p2', EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, 'fetch', adapter='s2', http_status=200)
758	    r = derive_route_status(L.events('p2'))
759	    check('route with 1/3 adapters answered is NOT complete',
760	          r.state == 'INCOMPLETE' and set(r.missing) == {'openalex', 'arxiv'}, f'got {r.state} {r.missing}')
761	
762	    # an attempt that never returned is a HANG, not an absence
763	    L.emit('p2', EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, 'fetch', adapter='openalex')
764	    L.emit('p2', EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, 'fetch', adapter='arxiv')
765	    r = derive_route_status(L.events('p2'))
766	    check('adapters ATTEMPTED but never answered are still MISSING (a hang is not a gap)',
767	          r.state == 'INCOMPLETE' and set(r.missing) == {'openalex', 'arxiv'}, f'got {r.state} {r.missing}')
768	
769	    # 4. a budget stop is not an evidence gap.
770	    L = Ledger()
771	    L.emit('p3', EventKind.ROUTE_PLANNED, 'router', adapters=['s2'])
772	    L.emit('p3', EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, 'fetch', adapter='s2')
773	    L.emit('p3', EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, 'fetch', adapter='s2', http_status=200)
774	    L.emit('p3', EventKind.BUDGET_STOPPED, 'driver', spent_usd=4.0, cap_usd=4.0)
775	    st, _ = derive_coverage_status(L, 'p3')
776	    check('a BUDGET STOP -> SEARCH_FAILED, not SEARCHED_NONE', st == SEARCH_FAILED, f'got {st}')
777	
778	    # 5. a clean, complete route with nothing found DOES license absence.
779	    L = Ledger()
780	    L.emit('p4', EventKind.ROUTE_PLANNED, 'router', adapters=['s2', 'openalex'])
781	    for a in ('s2', 'openalex'):
782	        L.emit('p4', EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, 'fetch', adapter=a)
783	        L.emit('p4', EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, 'fetch', adapter=a, http_status=200, n_results=0)
784	    st, _ = derive_coverage_status(L, 'p4')
785	    check('an ADEQUATE route with zero results -> SEARCHED_NONE (a scoped absence IS sayable)',
786	          st == SEARCHED_NONE, f'got {st}')
787	
788	    # 6. 404 is a fact about their index, not the world.
789	    L = Ledger()
790	    L.emit('p5', EventKind.ROUTE_PLANNED, 'router', adapters=['s2'])
791	    L.emit('p5', EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, 'fetch', adapter='s2')
792	    L.emit('p5', EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, 'fetch', adapter='s2', http_status=404)
793	    check('HTTP 404 -> NOT_INDEXED_BY_THIS_BACKEND (not "no free copy exists")',
794	          derive_backend_outcome(L.events('p5'), 's2') == NOT_INDEXED)
795	
796	    # 7. CONFLICTED and THIN are correct answers, not failures.
797	    #    Note what the component emits: a source_unit and a DIRECTION (an observation — the sign of
798	    #    the reported effect). It does NOT get to say the source is admissible. The reducer goes and
799	    #    re-derives that from each source's own observations, below.
800	    L = Ledger()
801	    L.emit('c1', EventKind.ROUTE_PLANNED, 'router', adapters=['s2'])
802	    L.emit('c1', EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, 'fetch', adapter='s2')
803	    L.emit('c1', EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, 'fetch', adapter='s2', http_status=200)
804	    for src, direction, words in (('10.1/a', 'positive', 9000), ('10.1/b', 'negative', 8000)):
805	        L.emit('c1', EventKind.CANDIDATE_IDENTIFIED, 'miner', source_unit=src, direction=direction)
806	        L.emit(src, EventKind.MANIFESTATION_FETCHED, 'fetch', url='u',
807	               requested_title='Robots and Jobs', requested_authors=['Acemoglu'])
808	        L.emit(src, EventKind.CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED, 'observer',
809	               **observe_text('Robots and Jobs by Acemoglu and Restrepo. ' + 'word ' * words))
810	    st, info = derive_coverage_status(L, 'c1')
811	    check('two admissible sources that disagree -> CONFLICTED', st == CONFLICTED, f'got {st} {info}')
812	
813	    # 7b. a cell whose every candidate is INADMISSIBLE must not read as "the literature is silent".
814	    L = Ledger()
815	    L.emit('c2', EventKind.ROUTE_PLANNED, 'router', adapters=['s2'])
816	    L.emit('c2', EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, 'fetch', adapter='s2')
817	    L.emit('c2', EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, 'fetch', adapter='s2', http_status=200)
818	    L.emit('c2', EventKind.CANDIDATE_IDENTIFIED, 'miner', source_unit='10.1/junk', direction='positive')
819	    L.emit('10.1/junk', EventKind.MANIFESTATION_FETCHED, 'fetch', url='u',
820	           requested_title='Rise of the Machines', requested_authors=['Parry'])
821	    L.emit('10.1/junk', EventKind.CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED, 'observer',
822	           **observe_text('This website uses cookies. By clicking the "Accept" button you agree. '
823	                          + 'word ' * 300))
824	    st, info = derive_coverage_status(L, 'c2')
825	    check('every candidate inadmissible -> SEARCHED_NONE that says so ("about OUR EVIDENCE")',
826	          st == SEARCHED_NONE and info['n_rejected'] == 1 and 'OUR EVIDENCE' in info['reason'],
827	          f'got {st} {info}')
828	
829	    # 8. weight components never collapse to a scalar.
830	    L = Ledger()
831	    L.emit('w1', EventKind.WEIGHT_COMPONENTS_DERIVED, 'scorer',
832	           directness='high', directness_provenance='span names the outcome variable',
833	           method_quality='low', method_quality_provenance='cross-section, no identification',
834	           influence_percentile=0.92, influence_percentile_provenance='4,743 citations (S2)')
835	    w = derive_weight_components(L.events('w1'))
836	    check('weights render as COMPONENTS with provenance, never as "high_quality"',
837	          not hasattr(w, 'label') and 'directness=high' in w.render() and 'method_quality=low' in w.render(),
838	          w.render())
839	    print(f'         {w.render()}')
840	
841	    print(f'\n  {ok} passed, {fail} failed')
842	    return 1 if fail else 0
843

846	# REPLAY — our REAL history, through the ledger. What SHOULD the labels have been?
847	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
848	
849	def _row(rows, author, year):
850	    for r in rows:
851	        if (r.get('authors') or ['?'])[0] == author and r.get('year') == year:
852	            return r
853	    raise KeyError(f'{author} {year}')
854	
855	
856	def replay() -> int:
857	    if not CORPUS.exists():
858	        print(f'!! corpus not found: {CORPUS}')
859	        return 1
860	    rows = json.loads(CORPUS.read_text())
861	
862	    print('\n' + '=' * 98)
863	    print('REPLAY OF OUR REAL HISTORY — the label we shipped vs the label the events earn')
864	    print('=' * 98)
865	    print(f'corpus: {CORPUS}  ({len(rows)} papers)')
866	
867	    caught = []
868	
869	    # ── CASE 1 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
870	    # Autor (2015), JEP. We stamped FULLTEXT. It is 535 words of aeaweb.org COOKIE BANNER.

876	        L.emit(u, EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, 'wp_fetch', adapter=a)
877	        L.emit(u, EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, 'wp_fetch', adapter=a, http_status=200)

879	    L.emit(u, EventKind.MANIFESTATION_FETCHED, 'wp_fetch', url=r.get('oa_url', ''),

881	    L.emit(u, EventKind.CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED, 'observer', **observe_text(r.get('fulltext') or ''))
882	    cls, cinfo = derive_content_profile(L.events(u))

884	    caught.append(dict(
885	        case='Autor (2015) — Journal of Economic Perspectives', doi=u,
886	        shipped='content_status = FULLTEXT   (fulltext_source = working_paper)',
887	        derived=f'{cls} -> {elig}',
888	        why=cinfo['reason'],
889	        proof=f'the 535 "words": {(r.get("fulltext") or "")[:96]!r}',
890	        events=len(L)))
891	
892	    # ── CASE 2 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
893	    # Parry (2016), Group & Organization Management. The title search matched SOMEBODY ELSE'S PAPER.
894	    r = _row(rows, 'Parry', 2016)
895	    L = Ledger()
896	    u = r['doi']
897	    L.emit(u, EventKind.ROUTE_PLANNED, 'wp_fetch', adapters=['s2/doi', 'openalex', 's2/title', 'arxiv'])
898	    for a, code in (('s2/doi', 404), ('openalex', 404), ('s2/title', 200), ('arxiv', 200)):
899	        L.emit(u, EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, 'wp_fetch', adapter=a)
900	        L.emit(u, EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, 'wp_fetch', adapter=a, http_status=code)
901	    L.emit(u, EventKind.CANDIDATE_IDENTIFIED, 'wp_fetch', adapter='s2/title',
902	           url='https://arxiv.org/pdf/2511.17203', matched_on='title substring "rise of the machines"')
903	    L.emit(u, EventKind.MANIFESTATION_FETCHED, 'wp_fetch', url='https://arxiv.org/pdf/2511.17203',
904	           requested_title=r['title'], requested_authors=r.get('authors', []),
905	           document_title='MATHEMATICS: THE RISE OF THE MACHINES', byline='YANG-HUI HE')
906	    L.emit(u, EventKind.CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED, 'observer', **observe_text(r.get('fulltext') or ''))
907	    binding, binfo = derive_semantic_binding(L.events(u))
908	    elig, einfo = derive_eligibility(L.events(u))
909	    caught.append(dict(
910	        case='Parry (2016) — Group & Organization Management', doi=u,
911	        shipped='content_status = FULLTEXT, 4,027w  (fulltext_source = working_paper)',
912	        derived=f'{binding} -> {elig}',
913	        why=binfo['reason'],
914	        proof='we hold Yang-Hui He, "Mathematics: The Rise of the Machines" (arXiv, 2025) — a paper '
915	              'on theorem-proving — filed under an HR journal article by Parry et al. (2016)',
916	        events=len(L)))
917	
918	    # ── CASE 3 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
919	    # Autor/Levy/Murnane (2003), QJE. "no free text" — the sentence that is four worlds at once.
920	    r = _row(rows, 'Autor', 2003)
921	    L = Ledger()
922	    u = r['doi']
923	    L.emit(u, EventKind.ROUTE_PLANNED, 'wp_fetch', adapters=['s2/doi', 'openalex', 's2/title', 'arxiv'])
924	    # what actually happens, verified live: S2 does not resolve this DOI. jget turns it into None.
925	    L.emit(u, EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, 'wp_fetch', adapter='s2/doi')
926	    L.emit(u, EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, 'wp_fetch', adapter='s2/doi', http_status=404,
927	           note='live probe 2026-07-13: S2 returned 404 for this DOI')
928	    L.emit(u, EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, 'wp_fetch', adapter='openalex')
929	    L.emit(u, EventKind.THROTTLED, 'wp_fetch', adapter='openalex', http_status=429,
930	           note='we hammered OpenAlex all night with repeated fetch runs')
931	    L.emit(u, EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, 'wp_fetch', adapter='s2/title')
932	    L.emit(u, EventKind.THROTTLED, 'wp_fetch', adapter='s2/title', http_status=429)
933	    # arxiv: attempted, never came back. THAT IS A HANG, and it looked exactly like a gap.
934	    L.emit(u, EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, 'wp_fetch', adapter='arxiv')
935	    route = derive_route_status(L.events(u))
936	    cov, cinfo = derive_coverage_status(L, u)
937	    caught.append(dict(
938	        case='Autor, Levy & Murnane (2003) — QJE  [the "no free text" line]', doi=u,
939	        shipped='log: "no free text"    (read by us as: no free copy of this paper exists)',
940	        derived=f'route={route.state}  coverage={cov}',
941	        why=cinfo['reason'] + f'; outcomes: ' +
942	            ', '.join(f'{a}={o}' for a, o in route.outcomes.items()),
943	        proof='and the paper IS free — NBER Working Paper 8337, in full, forever. "no free text" was '
944	              'false in every reading: 404 = their index, 429 = our request rate, no-outcome = a hang',
945	        events=len(L)))
946	
947	    # ── CASE 4 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
948	    # Acemoglu & Restrepo (2019), JEP. Labelled working_paper. IT IS THE JOURNAL ARTICLE.
949	    # The reducer must not be a sledgehammer: a blanket quarantine would DESTROY real evidence.
950	    r = _row(rows, 'Acemoglu', 2019)
951	    L = Ledger()
952	    u = r['doi']
953	    L.emit(u, EventKind.ROUTE_PLANNED, 'wp_fetch', adapters=['s2/doi'])
954	    L.emit(u, EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, 'wp_fetch', adapter='s2/doi')
955	    L.emit(u, EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, 'wp_fetch', adapter='s2/doi', http_status=200)
956	    L.emit(u, EventKind.MANIFESTATION_FETCHED, 'wp_fetch', url=r.get('oa_url', ''),
957	           requested_title=r['title'], requested_authors=r.get('authors', []))
958	    L.emit(u, EventKind.CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED, 'observer', **observe_text(r.get('fulltext') or ''))
959	    binding, binfo = derive_semantic_binding(L.events(u))
960	    elig, einfo = derive_eligibility(L.events(u))
961	    prof = observe_text(r.get('fulltext') or '')
962	    caught.append(dict(
963	        case='Acemoglu & Restrepo (2019) — JEP  [the label was false in the OTHER direction]', doi=u,
964	        shipped='fulltext_source = working_paper  (so a blanket quarantine would DELETE it)',
965	        derived=f'{binding} -> {elig}',
966	        why=f'header reads "Journal of Economic Perspectives—Volume 33, Number 2—Spring 2019—Pages 3–30". '
967	            f'It IS the article. The "working_paper" label named the SCRIPT, not the document. '
968	            f'(NBER appears at char 74,747 — in the REFERENCES.)',
969	        proof=f'preprint stamp in header: {prof["preprint_stamp_in_header"]}   '
970	              f'anywhere in text: {prof["preprint_stamp_anywhere"]}  <- a whole-text scan reads the '
971	              f'BIBLIOGRAPHY and calls the JEP article a working paper',
972	        events=len(L)))
973	
974	    # ── CASE 5 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
975	    # The counts that describe a document we threw away.
976	    trunc = [x for x in rows if x.get('fulltext_words')
977	             and x['fulltext_words'] != len((x.get('fulltext') or '').split())]
978	
979	    # ── CASE 6 — THE DECISIVE ONE ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
980	    # Sol's P0 says working-paper text is filed under journal DOIs. It is. But the label that is
981	    # supposed to mark it, `fulltext_source='working_paper'`, is written by WHICHEVER SCRIPT RAN —
982	    # so we tested it against what the documents ACTUALLY ARE.
983	    marked, truly_preprint, binding_of = set(), set(), {}
984	    for r in rows:
985	        if r.get('content_status') != 'FULLTEXT':
986	            continue
987	        key = f'{(r.get("authors") or ["?"])[0]} {r.get("year")}'
988	        if r.get('fulltext_source') == 'working_paper':

990	        L = Ledger()
991	        u = r.get('doi') or r.get('title', '?')
992	        L.emit(u, EventKind.MANIFESTATION_FETCHED, 'corpus', url=r.get('oa_url', ''),
993	               requested_title=r.get('title', ''), requested_authors=r.get('authors', []))
994	        L.emit(u, EventKind.CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED, 'observer',
995	               **observe_text(r.get('fulltext') or ''))
996	        b, _ = derive_semantic_binding(L.events(u))
997	        e, _ = derive_eligibility(L.events(u))
998	        binding_of[key] = (b, e)
999	        if b == VERSION_PREPRINT:
1000	            truly_preprint.add(key)
1001	
1002	    # ── render ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1003	    for i, c in enumerate(caught, 1):
1004	        print(f'\n┌─ CASE {i}: {c["case"]}')
1005	        print(f'│  doi        {c["doi"]}')
1006	        print(f'│  WE SHIPPED {c["shipped"]}')
1007	        print(f'│  LEDGER SAYS{"":1}{c["derived"]}')
1008	        print(f'│  because    {c["why"]}')
1009	        print(f'│  proof      {c["proof"]}')
1010	        print(f'└─ ({c["events"]} events)')
1011	
1012	    print('\n┌─ CASE 5: four word-counts that describe text we no longer hold')
1013	    for x in trunc:
1014	        au = (x.get('authors') or ['?'])[0]
1015	        print(f'│  {au[:12]:<12}{x["year"]}  claims {x["fulltext_words"]:>6,}w   holds '
1016	              f'{len((x.get("fulltext") or "").split()):>6,}w   (truncated at 120,000 chars)')
1017	    print('└─ the count was taken BEFORE the truncation and never re-derived')
1018	
1019	    # Count ONLY what the evidence supports: the marked rows that are genuinely ADMISSIBLE journal
1020	    # articles. ("marked but not a preprint" would include the cookie banner and the stranger's
1021	    # paper — calling those "genuine journal articles" would be this project's own disease, in the
1022	    # summary line of its cure.)
1023	    would_destroy = sorted(k for k in marked if binding_of.get(k, ('', ''))[1] == ADMISSIBLE)
1024	
1025	    print('\n┌─ CASE 6: THE LABEL THAT IS SUPPOSED TO CATCH SOL\'S P0 — TESTED AGAINST THE DOCUMENTS')
1026	    print(f'│  rows the LABEL `fulltext_source=working_paper` marks : {len(marked)}')

input.old_string:
NO UNIVERSAL WORD THRESHOLD (Sol, item 4)

  "A universal word threshold cannot define 'full text': a short judicial opinion, statute section,
   trial-registry record, and journal article have different completeness profiles."

  MIN_WORDS=2500 was my own invention and it is deleted. `profile()` derives, FROM THE BYTES:
  artifact_kind, sections present, body/chrome ratio, result-bearing sections, extractability,
  identity agreement, fetch outcome — and judges completeness AGAINST THE PROFILE FOR THAT KIND.
  A four-page judicial opinion is COMPLETE. A 535-word cookie banner is not, at any length.

  "A universal word threshold cannot define 'full text': a short judicial opinion, statute section,
   trial-registry record, and journal article have different completeness profiles."

  MIN_WORDS=2500 was my own invention and it is deleted. `profile()` derives, FROM THE BYTES:
  artifact_kind, sections present, body/chrome ratio, result-bearing sections, extractability,
  identity agreement, fetch outcome — and judges completeness AGAINST THE PROFILE FOR THAT KIND.
  A four-page judicial opinion is COMPLETE. A 535-word cookie banner is not, at any length.

  There is exactly ONE reducer that may answer "is this the whole document?": `judge_completeness()`,
  driven by the KIND_PROFILE registry. `profile()` calls it, `Graph.from_json()` calls it, and
  `event_ledger.derive_content_profile()` calls it. It was written twice before — 1,200 words here
  and FULLTEXT_MIN=2,500 there — which is not a shared rule, it is two rules that disagree, and the
  document a card cites depends on which module last looked at it.

THE API. EXACTLY THREE ENTRY POINTS (Sol, "reality checks", item 5)

    bind_span(manifestation_id, start, end)   -> bytes -> a bound span. BOUNDS ARE VALIDATED.
    resolve_attribution(manifestation_id, policy) -> what that span may NAME, under THIS task's rule
    verify_span(binding)                      -> the enforcement point re-checks EVERYTHING

  bind_span() returns IDENTIFIERS — `expression_id` and `permitted_expression_ids`. It used to return
  `may_name`: a list of English SENTENCES. A sentence is a display cache, and a consumer that must
  string-match prose to decide what is legal has no rule at all — it has a suggestion. Prose is
  produced by resolve_attribution(), carried as `.text`, and NOTHING IS EVER VALIDATED AGAINST IT.

# The version taxonomy, exactly as specified. `repository` is NOT a version — a repository copy is a
# MANIFESTATION (a place bytes live) of some expression, which is where it sits in the graph below.
EXPRESSION_KINDS = ('journal_version', 'accepted_manuscript', 'working_paper', 'preprint', 'unknown')

# Artifact kinds carry their OWN completeness profile. This list is deliberately wider than this
# corpus needs: the moment `full text` is defined by a number of words, a short judicial opinion
# becomes "an abstract" and a cookie banner becomes "a paper".
ARTIFACT_KINDS = (
    'journal_article', 'working_paper', 'preprint', 'accepted_manuscript',
    'judicial_opinion', 'statute_section', 'trial_registry_record',
    'abstract', 'landing_page', 'extraction_failure', 'wrong_work', 'unknown',
)


@dataclass
class Work:
    """The research object / case / trial. NOT a document. Nothing binds to it."""
    id: str
    title: str
    authors: list[str]
    year: int | None
    venue: str | None            # the venue of the *journal expression*, if one is claimed
    doi: str | None
    kind: str = 'study'


@dataclass
class Expression:
    """A VERSION of the report of a Work. This is the unit an attribution may name."""
    id: str
    work_id: str
    kind: str                     # EXPRESSION_KINDS
    kind_basis: str               # WHY we believe that — derived from bytes, or 'claimed_by_metadata'
    attribution: str              # the sentence a writer is allowed to write for THIS expression
    citable_in_journal_only_answer: bool
input.new_string:
# The version taxonomy. `repository` is NOT a version — a repository copy is a MANIFESTATION (a place
# bytes live) of some expression, which is where it sits in the graph below.
#
# A JUDICIAL OPINION HAS NO `journal_version`, AND A STATUTE HAS NO PREPRINT. The scholarly five were
# the only kinds here, and `migrate()` therefore minted a `journal_version` for every row it saw —
# which is not a taxonomy, it is an assumption wearing one. `official_text` and `registry_record` are
# the authoritative expressions of the non-scholarly works, and the registry below says which work
# kind has which.
EXPRESSION_KINDS = ('journal_version', 'proceedings_version', 'accepted_manuscript', 'working_paper',
                    'preprint', 'official_text', 'registry_record', 'unknown')


@dataclass
class Work:
    """The research object / case / trial. NOT a document. Nothing binds to it.

    `kind` IS LOAD-BEARING, not decoration: it selects the completeness profile its documents are
    judged against. A `case` is judged as a judicial opinion — COMPLETE AT ANY LENGTH — and a `study`
    is judged as a scholarly article, where 300 words is a fragment. Judge one by the other's rule and
    you either destroy the opinion or admit the fragment.
    """
    id: str
    title: str
    authors: list[str]
    year: int | None
    venue: str | None            # the venue / court / reporter / registry that names the official text
    doi: str | None
    kind: str = 'study'          # WORK_KINDS


@dataclass
class Expression:
    """A VERSION of the report of a Work. This is the unit an attribution may name.

    There is NO `citable_in_journal_only_answer` field any more. Citability is a property of THE
    ANSWER'S INSTRUCTION, not of the node: the same accepted manuscript is inadmissible under
    "journal articles only" and admissible under "any identified version". A boolean stored on the
    node has to be wrong for one of them, and a stored label that can be wrong is this whole file's
    subject. It is DERIVED, per policy, by `Graph.resolve_attribution()`.
    """
    id: str
    work_id: str
    kind: str                     # EXPRESSION_KINDS
    kind_basis: str               # WHY we believe that — derived from bytes, or 'claimed_by_metadata'
    attribution: str              # DISPLAY ONLY. The sentence a writer may render for THIS expression.
    #                             # Nothing is ever validated against it — see THE LAW.

# --- what "complete" MEANS, per artifact kind ----------------------------------------------------
# Sol's point, made mechanical: completeness is a property OF A KIND, never of a word count.
#
# `stub_floor` separates THE DOCUMENT from A STUB OF IT, and it exists ONLY for the scholarly kinds,
# where a 500-word artifact is definitionally not the article (it is its abstract, or its landing
# page). For a judicial opinion, a statute section or a trial-registry record it is None: THOSE
# ARTIFACTS ARE COMPLETE AT ANY LENGTH. That is the whole difference between this and MIN_WORDS=2500,
# which asserted one number over every kind of document in the world.
#
# `needs_results` IS DELIBERATELY ABSENT. I first wrote it as a completeness gate and it declared six
# genuine journal articles incomplete — Technovation (15,985w), JEP (11,872w), AER (9,732w), JBR,
# Raj, Chalmers — because economics papers title their results sections after their CONTENT ("V. The
# Effect of Robots on Employment"), and a JEP essay has no results section at all. Gating on a
# section heading is the same error as gating on a word count, moved one axis over. The
# result-bearing sections are REPORTED in the profile (the miner weights by them) and GATE NOTHING.
KIND_PROFILE = {
    'journal_article':      dict(stub_floor=1200),
    'working_paper':        dict(stub_floor=1200),
    'preprint':             dict(stub_floor=1200),
    'accepted_manuscript':  dict(stub_floor=1200),
    # A short official opinion IS the whole artifact. No results section, no methods, and COMPLETE.
    'judicial_opinion':     dict(stub_floor=None),
    'statute_section':      dict(stub_floor=None),
    'trial_registry_record': dict(stub_floor=None),
    # These are NEVER complete, at any length, and may never carry a finding.
    'abstract':             dict(stub_floor=None),
    'landing_page':         dict(stub_floor=None),
    'extraction_failure':   dict(stub_floor=None),
    'wrong_work':           dict(stub_floor=None),
    'unknown':              dict(stub_floor=None),
}
# Kinds whose text may carry a finding at all, IF the profile also says complete.
FINDING_BEARING_KINDS = ('journal_article', 'working_paper', 'preprint', 'accepted_manuscript',
                         'judicial_opinion', 'statute_section', 'trial_registry_record')

RESULT_BEARING = ('results', 'tables', 'analysis')

# The scholarly stub floor, used ONLY to decide document-vs-stub for the scholarly kinds above.
SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR = 1200

#: artifact kind is derived from the bytes' own version furniture. Everything else is fixed by the
#: work: a case produces judicial opinions, and no amount of NBER letterhead makes one a working paper.
WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT: dict[str, str | None] = {
    'study':   None,
    'case':    'judicial_opinion',
    'statute': 'statute_section',
    'trial':   'trial_registry_record',
}
WORK_KINDS = tuple(WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT)

#: THE ROW'S OWN DECLARED TYPE -> (work kind, the expression the METADATA claims exists).
#: `migrate()` used to hardcode a `journal_version` for every row in the corpus. Sol: "That is not
#: valid for a judicial opinion or statute." The row DECLARES its type — every one of the 70 rows here
#: carries `type: 'journal-article'` from Crossref, and the code never once read it. It assumed the
#: answer it happened to be right about, which is not the same as being right.
SOURCE_TYPE: dict[str, tuple[str, str]] = {
    'journal-article':     ('study',   'journal_version'),
    'journal article':     ('study',   'journal_version'),
    'proceedings-article': ('study',   'proceedings_version'),
    'book-chapter':        ('study',   'journal_version'),
    'posted-content':      ('study',   'preprint'),      # a preprint row claims NO journal version
    'preprint':            ('study',   'preprint'),
    'report':              ('study',   'working_paper'),
    'working-paper':       ('study',   'working_paper'),
    'judicial-opinion':    ('case',    'official_text'),
    'opinion':             ('case',    'official_text'),
    'case':                ('case',    'official_text'),
    'statute':             ('statute', 'official_text'),
    'legislation':         ('statute', 'official_text'),
    'clinical-trial':      ('trial',   'registry_record'),
    'trial-registration':  ('trial',   'registry_record'),
}

RESULT_BEARING = ('results', 'tables', 'analysis')


def judge_completeness(artifact_kind: str, n_words: int,
                       extraction_verdict: str = 'CLEAN') -> tuple[bool, list[str]]:
    """THE ONE COMPLETENESS REDUCER. `profile()`, `Graph.from_json()` and
    `event_ledger.derive_content_profile()` all come through here and through nothing else.

    THERE IS NO UNIVERSAL WORD THRESHOLD IN THIS FUNCTION AND THERE MUST NEVER BE ONE. Completeness is
    read out of KIND_PROFILE for THIS kind: a judicial opinion, a statute section and a trial-registry
    record have stub_floor=None and are COMPLETE AT ANY LENGTH. A cookie banner is incomplete at ANY
    length, because `landing_page` cannot bear a finding at all — which is the correct reason, and not
    a fact about its size.

    The one length that is never enough is ZERO. "Complete at any length" is not complete at no length:
    a registry record we hold nothing of is not a short registry record.
    """
    spec = KIND_PROFILE.get(artifact_kind)
    if spec is None:
        raise ValueError(f'{artifact_kind!r} is not a registered artifact kind — an unregistered kind '
                         f'cannot be judged complete OR incomplete, and defaulting it to either is how '
                         f'a label outruns its evidence. Register it in KIND_PROFILE.')
    reasons: list[str] = []
    if not spec['finding_bearing']:
        reasons.append(f'artifact kind `{artifact_kind}` cannot carry a finding')
    if extraction_verdict == 'CORRUPT':
        reasons.append('bytes are not readable prose')
    if n_words <= 0:
        reasons.append('there are no bytes — complete at any length is not complete at NO length')
    elif spec['stub_floor'] is not None and n_words < spec['stub_floor']:
        reasons.append(f'{n_words} words is below the stub floor for `{artifact_kind}` '
                       f'({spec["stub_floor"]}) — this is a fragment of the document, not the document')
    return (not reasons), reasons

def derive_expression_kind(text: str, work_kind: str = 'study') -> tuple[str, str]:
    """Which VERSION do these bytes say they are? Derived from self-identifying furniture.

    Returns ('unknown', ...) freely. `unknown` is the honest answer for a PDF body with its title
    page mangled, and an honest `unknown` costs us evidence -- which is the correct price.

    THE SCHOLARLY VERSION TAXONOMY APPLIES ONLY TO SCHOLARLY WORKS. There is no preprint of a statute
    and no accepted manuscript of a judicial opinion; the authoritative text IS the artifact, and the
    registry says so. Running the NBER/arXiv/"accepted manuscript" regexes over a court opinion does
    not find nothing — it finds whatever the opinion happens to QUOTE, which is how a whole-text scan
    for "NBER Working Paper" once read the bibliography and called the JEP article a working paper.
    """
    fam = WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT.get(work_kind or 'study')
    if fam is not None:
        ex = KIND_PROFILE[fam]['expression']
        return ex, (f'work kind `{work_kind}` — the version taxonomy of a {fam.replace("_", " ")} is '
                    f'not the scholarly one; these bytes are its {ex.replace("_", " ")}')
    head = text[:12000]

262	# `official_text`; there is no journal version of it to widen to, ever.
263	#

@dataclass
class Graph:
    works: dict[str, Work] = field(default_factory=dict)
    expressions: dict[str, Expression] = field(default_factory=dict)
    manifestations: dict[str, Manifestation] = field(default_factory=dict)
    edges: list[Edge] = field(default_factory=list)

    # -- construction -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    def add_edge(self, src: str, dst: str, type: str, status: str, basis: str) -> Edge:
        if type not in EDGE_TYPES:
            raise ValueError(f'unknown edge type {type!r}')
        if status not in ('PROPOSED', 'ASSERTED'):
            raise ValueError(f'edge status must be PROPOSED or ASSERTED, not {status!r}')
        if not basis or not basis.strip():
            # An edge with no evidence is the entire failure we are here to end.
            raise ValueError('an edge MUST carry a basis naming the evidence for it')
        if status == 'ASSERTED' and type == 'exact_copy_of' and 'title' in basis.lower():
            # Sol, verbatim: a title similarity match can PROPOSE predecessor_of; it CANNOT ASSERT
            # exact_copy_of. Enforced in the constructor so no caller can talk its way past it.
            raise ValueError('a title match may not ASSERT exact_copy_of — it proves nothing about bytes')
        e = Edge(src, dst, type, status, basis)
        self.edges.append(e)
        return e

    # -- THE CORE RULE ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    def attribution_targets(self, manif_id: str) -> list[str]:
        """Which expressions a span in this manifestation MAY name. Sol's rule, mechanised.

        Always: the manifestation's own expression -- the bytes we hold are, definitionally, that
        expression's bytes. Additionally: any expression reachable by an ASSERTED, SPAN-PRESERVING
        edge, because such an edge is exactly a proof that the cited source contains the same span.
        Nothing else. A `predecessor_of` edge, however obvious, transfers NOTHING.
        """
        m = self.manifestations[manif_id]
        targets = [m.expression_id]
        for e in self.edges:
            if e.src == m.expression_id and e.type in SPAN_PRESERVING and e.status == 'ASSERTED':
                targets.append(e.dst)
        return targets

    def journal_attributable(self, manif_id: str) -> bool:
        """May a span from these bytes be printed as a finding OF A JOURNAL ARTICLE?

        Two independent conditions, BOTH required:
          1. some expression this span may legally name is a journal_version, AND
          2. the bytes are actually usable as evidence (complete, uncorrupted, the right work).
        A span from a corrupt PDF of the real journal article is still inadmissible -- see Weiss.
        """
        m = self.manifestations[manif_id]
        if not m.profile.get('complete'):
            return False
        return any(self.expressions[t].kind == 'journal_version'
                   for t in self.attribution_targets(manif_id))

    def attribution_for(self, manif_id: str) -> str:
        """The ONLY sentence a writer may use for a span from these bytes."""
        m = self.manifestations[manif_id]
        return self.expressions[m.expression_id].attribution

    # -- span binding -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    def bind_span(self, manif_id: str, start: int, end: int) -> dict:
        """A span binds to its EXACT manifestation and content hash. This is what a card must carry
        instead of a bare DOI -- a DOI names a Work, and a Work has no bytes."""
        m = self.manifestations[manif_id]
        return dict(manifestation_id=manif_id, content_hash=m.content_hash,
                    span_start=start, span_end=end,
                    text=m.text[start:end],
                    may_name=[self.expressions[t].attribution for t in self.attribution_targets(manif_id)],
                    journal_attributable=self.journal_attributable(manif_id))

    def verify_span(self, bound: dict) -> bool:
        """The bytes a span was mined from must still be the bytes we hold."""
        m = self.manifestations.get(bound['manifestation_id'])
        return bool(m) and m.content_hash == bound['content_hash'] \
            and m.text[bound['span_start']:bound['span_end']] == bound['text']

    def to_json(self) -> dict:
        return dict(
            works=[asdict(w) for w in self.works.values()],
            expressions=[asdict(e) for e in self.expressions.values()],
            # text is retained on disk too: NOTHING IS EVER DELETED.
            manifestations=[asdict(m) for m in self.manifestations.values()],
            edges=[asdict(e) for e in self.edges],
        )
